chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR Const. Pett No.D-543 of 2022 DATE OF HEARING ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE For hearing of main case 15.09.2025 Mr. Ghulam Abbas Akhter, Advocate for respondents/SEPCO Syed Naveed Ahmed Shah, Deputy Attorney General ************** On last date of hearing viz.08.09.2025, the following order was passed:- “None present for the petitioners nor any intimation received. This petition was lastly fixed in Court on 02.06.2022, thereafter no one has turn up to pursue the instant petition. The conduct of the petitioner and their counsel prima-facie suggests that they are no more interested to proceed with this petition. In the circumstances, this petition is liable to be dismissed for non-prosecution; however in the interest of justice, the matter is adjourned to 15.09.2025. Let intimation notice be issued to the counsel for the petitioner for the next date of hearing” Though the intimation notice was issued to counsel for the petitioner despite of that none present for petitioner nor any intimation received. The record reflects that the petitioner and his counsel are not interested in pursuing the matter. Accordingly, the present Constitution Petition stands dismissed for non-prosecution. J U D G E J U D G E M. Ali*
